policy.goal: Store machine-validatable migration-readiness packets for Telegram runtimes before Aiogram 3 implementation gates open.
packet_required_for: shared Telegram primitive extraction from product runtimes
implementation_open_requires: matching backlog surface and primary role
complete security controls and secret classifications
recorded validation plan and rollback plan
policy.secret_policy: Registry stores classifications only. Tokens, webhook secrets, chat IDs, supplier secrets, and raw payloads are forbidden.
policy.deprecated_projects_refs: Any retained projects/* packet path is legacy compatibility inventory only and is not canonical project authority; canonical project registry authority is /srv/bears/dev/registry/projects.v1.json.

validation_plan: Run product unit tests and MCP stdio smoke from /srv/bears/dev/app/codex-telegram without live Telegram mutation.
Run platform role route/audit for /srv/bears/dev/app/codex-telegram before product changes.
Keep production registration pointed to the Kubernetes MCP endpoint only; do not register a dev runtime or local daemon.
rollback_plan: Rollback production by reverting the pinned Kubernetes image digest to the previous digest.
Keep /srv/bears/projects/codex-telegram as evidence only; never promote it back to active source authority.
Remove workspace-control MCP registration if the production endpoint fails health or auth checks.
missing_evidence: Production Kubernetes truth packet must be filled before any apply.
Production rollout evidence must be sanitized and uploaded by CD after apply.
Live Telegram send evidence is not required for local validation and needs explicit operator approval if requested.

legacy_compatibility.allowed_use: Evidence only; no active runtime or registry ownership.
